panels.description: Apdex is a measure of requests that complete within a tolerable period of time for the service. Higher is better.

matcher.options: /6h Degradation SLO \(5% of monthly error budget\).*/
/1h Outage SLO \(2% of monthly error budget\).*/

targets.legendFormat: 6h Degradation SLO (5% of monthly error budget)
1h Outage SLO (2% of monthly error budget)

panels.title: hosted-runners Service Apdex

targets.expr:
sum(
  label_replace(
    vector(0) and on() (gitlab_component_shard_apdex:ratio_1h{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"}),
    "period", "na", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(1) and on () (gitlab_component_shard_apdex:ratio_5m{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"} < on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (1 - (14.4 * (1 - slo:min:events:gitlab_service_apdex:ratio{component="",shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"})))),
    "period", "5m", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(2) and on () (gitlab_component_shard_apdex:ratio_1h{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"} < on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (1 - (14.4 * (1 - slo:min:events:gitlab_service_apdex:ratio{component="",shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"})))),
    "period", "1h", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(4) and on () (gitlab_component_shard_apdex:ratio_30m{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"} < on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (1 - (6 * (1 - slo:min:events:gitlab_service_apdex:ratio{component="",shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"})))),
    "period", "30m", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(8) and on () (gitlab_component_shard_apdex:ratio_6h{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"} < on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (1 - (6 * (1 - slo:min:events:gitlab_service_apdex:ratio{component="",shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"})))),
    "period", "6h", "", ""
  )
)

panels.description: Error rates are a measure of unhandled service exceptions per second. Client errors are excluded when possible. Lower is better

matcher.options: /6h Degradation SLO \(5% of monthly error budget\).*/
/1h Outage SLO \(2% of monthly error budget\).*/

targets.legendFormat: 6h Degradation SLO (5% of monthly error budget)
1h Outage SLO (2% of monthly error budget)

panels.title: hosted-runners Service Error Ratio

targets.expr:
sum(
  label_replace(
    vector(0) and on() (gitlab_component_shard_errors:ratio_1h{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"}),
    "period", "na", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(1) and on () (gitlab_component_shard_errors:ratio_5m{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (14.4 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="",shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"})),
    "period", "5m", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(2) and on () (gitlab_component_shard_errors:ratio_1h{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (14.4 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="",shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"})),
    "period", "1h", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(4) and on () (gitlab_component_shard_errors:ratio_30m{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (6 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="",shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"})),
    "period", "30m", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(8) and on () (gitlab_component_shard_errors:ratio_6h{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (6 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="",shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})",shard=~"$shard",type="hosted-runners"})),
    "period", "6h", "", ""
  )
)

panels.description: The operation rate is the sum total of all requests being handle for all components within this service. Note that a single user request can lead to requests to multiple components. Higher is busier.
panels.title: hosted-runners Service RPS - Requests per Second

panels.description: Current GitLab Runner version for each runner manager instance, including OS, architecture, and Go version.
panels.title: GitLab Runner(s) Versions

panels.description: Time elapsed since each runner manager process last started. Sorted ascending to surface recently restarted instances.
panels.title: GitLab Runner(s) Uptime

panels.description: Online: API requests in last 5 min. Offline: present in version_info but no API activity. Stale: seen within the last 24h but no longer reporting metrics.
panels.title: Runner Manager Status

panels.description: Number of jobs executed by this shard since it came up.
panels.title: Total Jobs Executed

panels.description: Number of jobs that have failed on this shard since it came up.
panels.title: Total Failed Jobs

panels.description: Number of jobs currently running on this shard.
panels.title: Jobs Running

panels.description: Number of concurrent jobs this shard can handle.
panels.title: Concurrent Job Limit

panels.description: Rate of errors caught by the runner manager process itself (not job failures), broken down by log level.
panels.title: Runner Manager Caught Errors

panels.description: Rate of all API calls from gitlab-runner to GitLab, broken down by endpoint, status code, and shard.
panels.title: Total number of api requests

panels.description: Current number of running jobs per shard.
panels.title: Jobs

panels.description: Distribution of currently running jobs by executor stage (prepare, build, cleanup, etc.)
panels.title: Running jobs phase

panels.description: Rate of job failures by failure reason.
panels.title: Failures on runners (by shard,failure_reason)

panels.description: Heatmap of job durations. A shift toward longer durations can indicate runner or infrastructure slowdowns.
panels.title: Finished job durations histogram

panels.description: Total job-minutes consumed per rate interval by shard. The red average line is sampled at a coarser interval to smooth out short-term fluctuations. Useful for tracking overall compute consumption trends.
panels.title: Finished job minutes increase

panels.description: Ratio of running jobs to the concurrent ceiling per shard, with soft (85%) and hard (90%) SLO thresholds. Values approaching 100% indicate the shard is near capacity.
panels.title: Runner saturation of concurrent by shard

panels.description: Ratio of running jobs to the limit ceiling per shard, with soft (85%) and hard (90%) SLO thresholds. Values approaching 100% indicate the shard is near capacity.
panels.title: Runner saturation of limit by shard

panels.description: ci_pending_builds is a global metric across the entire GitLab instance — it is not filtered by runner, shard, or stack. Shows the total number of CI builds waiting for a runner (where shared_runners="no").
panels.title: Global count of pending builds

panels.description: Number of jobs in the candidate list Rails returns to the runner on a request_job call, filtered to eligible jobs for this runner.
panels.title: Pending jobs queue size

panels.description: Distribution of time jobs spend in the queue before a runner accepts them. A widening or rising distribution indicates runners cannot keep up with the incoming job rate.
panels.title: Pending job queue duration histogram

panels.description: Average time jobs spend waiting in the queue before a runner picks them up, per shard. Hard SLO is 120 s (2 min). A rising trend indicates insufficient runner capacity.
panels.title: Average duration of queuing

panels.description: Count of jobs that waited longer than the acceptable queuing duration SLO threshold. Sustained non-zero values are worth investigating.
panels.title: Acceptable job queuing duration exceeded

panels.description: Percentage of total jobs that exceeded the acceptable queuing duration SLO threshold.
panels.title: Jobs queuing failure rate

panels.description: Rate of CI build queue operations (push, pop, etc.) across the GitLab instance. A growing gap between push and pop rates means jobs are accumulating faster than runners can consume them.
panels.title: Rate of builds queue operations

panels.description:
The number of iterations Rails performs over the initial job list before finding
a job that can be assigned to the requesting runner. A job may be skipped if it
has unmet requirements or was already assigned to another runner.

High depth relative to queue size means many jobs in the list are not eligible
for the runner, which increases latency for job assignment.
panels.title: Pending jobs queue depth

panels.description: Ratio of (running + creating + deleting) instances to max_instances. Approaching 100% means the pool is full and new VMs cannot be created until old ones are deleted.
panels.title: Fleeting instances saturation

panels.description: Count of fleeting instances in each lifecycle state (requested, creating, pending, running, deleting etc.). A growing "deleting" count without a drop in "running" can indicate stuck deletions.
panels.title: Fleeting instances states

panels.description: Ratio of active tasks (excluding idle/reserved) to total task capacity (max_instances × max_tasks_per_instance). High saturation means the taskscaler is running near its configured ceiling.
panels.title: Taskscaler tasks saturation

panels.description: Current task count by state (idle → reserved → acquired → released). Shows how demand is distributed across the task lifecycle.
panels.title: Taskscaler tasks

panels.description: Percentage of taskscaler instances sitting idle per shard. High idle ratios indicate over-provisioning — useful for cost and performance tuning.

targets.expr:
sum by(shard) (fleeting_taskscaler_tasks{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})", shard=~"$shard", state="idle"})
/
sum by(shard) (fleeting_taskscaler_tasks{shard=~".+-(${stack:pipe})", shard=~"$shard"})

panels.title: Taskscaler idle ratio

panels.description: Number of VMs the taskscaler currently wants to maintain based on demand. Compare with actual instance counts to identify scaling lag.
panels.title: Taskscaler desired instances

panels.description: Rate of provisioner instance lifecycle operations (create, delete, update, etc.) broken down by shard and operation type.
panels.title: Fleeting instance operations rate

panels.description: Rate of internal provisioner bookkeeping operations (e.g. reconciliation, state tracking) broken down by shard and operation type.
panels.title: Fleeting internal operations rate

panels.description: Rate of skipped provisioner reconciliation cycles. Should be near zero; sustained non-zero values indicate the provisioner cannot keep up with its update loop.
panels.title: Fleeting missed updates rate

panels.description: Rate of taskscaler task operations (acquire, release, etc.) broken down by shard and operation type.
panels.title: Taskscaler operations rate

panels.description:
Rate of taskscaler capacity reservation failures.

- **reserve_iop_capacity_failure**: zero capacity whatsoever — both available and potential are ≤ 0.
- **reserve_available_capacity_failure**: no immediately available capacity (available ≤ 0) but potential capacity exists (VMs could be created).
panels.title: Taskscaler operations failure

panels.description: Rate of scale-up and scale-down decisions made by the taskscaler. Imbalanced rates may indicate oscillation or a misconfigured scaling policy.
panels.title: Taskscaler scale operations rate

panels.description: Distribution of time taken to create a new VM. Increases typically point to cloud provider slowness or quota issues.
panels.title: Fleeting instance creation timing

panels.description: Distribution of time from VM creation to the instance reporting as running. Longer durations indicate slow VM boot or cloud provider delays.
panels.title: Fleeting instance is_running timing

panels.description: Distribution of time taken to delete a VM. Slow deletions reduce the rate at which capacity can be recycled.
panels.title: Fleeting instance deletion timing

panels.description: Distribution of total VM lifetime from creation to deletion. Useful for understanding instance turnover rate and whether VMs are being retired on schedule.
panels.title: Fleeting instance life duration

panels.description: Configured maximum number of tasks an instance will execute before being retired and replaced. Changes here reflect config updates.
panels.title: Taskscaler max use count per instance

panels.description: Distribution of time from VM creation until the taskscaler considers the instance ready to accept tasks.
panels.title: Taskscaler instance readiness timing

panels.description: Rate at which worker goroutines call GitLab to request a new job. A drop indicates runners have stopped polling — check connectivity and authentication.
panels.title: Worker feed rate

panels.description: Rate of failed poll attempts when workers request jobs from GitLab. Sustained non-zero values indicate a connectivity, token, or GitLab availability problem.
panels.title: Worker feed failures rate

panels.description: Current number of available worker slots. Each slot can hold one in-flight job; this should roughly track the configured concurrent setting.
panels.title: Worker slots

panels.description: Rate of worker slot acquire/release operations. High release rates relative to acquire rates can indicate rapid job failures.
panels.title: Worker slot operations rate

panels.description: Rate of job-processing failures broken down by failure_type (e.g. runner_system_failure, script_failure).
panels.title: Worker processing failures rate

panels.description: Rate of worker health-check failures by runner instance. These indicate a worker goroutine has become unhealthy or unable to communicate with the executor.
panels.title: Worker health check failures rate

panels.description: Hard limit (absolute ceiling), adaptive limit (dynamically adjusted), used limit (currently enforced), and actual requests in flight. In-flight should stay below the adaptive limit during normal operation.
panels.title: Request concurrency

panels.description: Count of times the request concurrency limit was hit, causing a request to wait or be dropped. Sustained non-zero values mean the runner is rate-limited on job polling.
panels.title: Request concurrency exceeded

panels.description: Rate of runner API calls to GitLab for the request_job endpoint, broken down by HTTP status code.
panels.title: Runner requests for request_job [.*]

panels.description: Rate of runner API calls to GitLab for the patch_trace endpoint, broken down by HTTP status code.
panels.title: Runner requests for patch_trace [.*]

panels.description: Rate of runner API calls to GitLab for the update_job endpoint, broken down by HTTP status code.
panels.title: Runner requests for update_job [.*]

panels.description: Overall rate of runner polling requests across all hosted-runner shards. A sustained drop typically means runners have stopped polling — check connectivity and authentication.
panels.title: Polling RPS - Overall

panels.description: Overall rate of errors returned on runner polling requests. Sustained non-zero values indicate a connectivity, token, or GitLab availability problem.
panels.title: Polling Error - Overall
